criteria.label: Define underfitting and overfitting using train vs validation error patterns.
criteria.example: Write: underfitting = high train + high val; overfitting = low train + much higher val gap.

criteria.label: Diagnose the regime from a reported train/val metric table.
criteria.example: Given train MSE 0.1 and val MSE 1.8 for a depth-20 tree, label overfitting and recommend max_depth reduction or more data.

criteria.label: Interpret a learning curve sketch for whether more data helps.
criteria.example: Sketch axes: val error falls as n grows while train stays low — conclude variance-limited overfitting; recommend more labels.

criteria.label: Choose an appropriate fix for underfitting on tabular data.
criteria.example: Propose switching from depth-1 stump to depth-6 trees or adding interaction features when train and val accuracy are both 60%.

criteria.label: Explain when early stopping beats adding model capacity.
criteria.example: Describe val loss rising after epoch 8 while train loss falls — stop at epoch 8 instead of widening the network.
